title: autobench.judge.pool.verdict v1
description: Per-case consensus verdict from an anonymous N-judge pool. Each case is scored by N independent JudgingPool members (default N=5 anonymous MiniMax instances per nervous-bus-c48). The pool's majority verdict, mean continuous scores, dissent ratio, and a compact per-judge vote summary are recorded here so downstream consumers (AHE, promotion, calibration) can distinguish high-confidence consensus from contested verdicts. Source: /autobench.

iteration.description: RSI iteration number (0 when not running in an RSI loop)

n_judges.description: Number of judges invoked for this case (target ensemble size, e.g. 5 by default)

n_votes.description: Number of judges that successfully returned a vote (some may have errored)

consensus_p_score.description: Mean p_score across all successful judge votes (continuous, [0, 1])

consensus_p_cost.description: Mean p_cost across all successful judge votes

consensus_p_time.description: Mean p_time across all successful judge votes

dissent_ratio.description: Fraction of votes that did NOT match the consensus verdict. 0.0 = unanimous agreement; values > the configured threshold (default 0.4) trigger an autobench.judge.disagreement.v1 escalation event.

verdict_distribution.description: Map of verdict-string -> count of judges that voted for it. Sums to n_votes.

votes_summary.description: Compact per-judge vote summary. Each element records one judge's anonymous slot, its verdict, and its p_score. Reasoning text is omitted to keep the bus payload bounded — full reasoning lives on the in-process JudgeVote dataclass and may be persisted by the integration layer if needed.

slot.description: Anonymous judge slot index (0-based), assigned at pool construction. Stable across cases within one run.
